summary: Fixed PostModal.js mobile touch responsiveness issue. Changed touch-none CSS class to touch-manipulation and simplified event handlers. Save button now uses simple onClick, reaction button uses unified pointer events.
critical_code_review_comments: Root cause: touch-none CSS class was blocking all touch events on mobile devices
Fix: Changed to touch-manipulation which allows tap/click while preventing scroll/zoom gestures
Save button simplified from complex touch/mouse handlers to simple onClick
Reaction button changed from separate touch/mouse handlers to unified pointer events (onPointerDown/onPointerUp)
Playwright tests pass - real mobile device testing recommended to confirm fix
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fixed mobile touch responsiveness in PostModal.js. Key changes: 1) touch-none -> touch-manipulation CSS class, 2) Save button uses simple onClick, 3) Reaction button uses pointer events. Playwright tests pass but real mobile device testing is recommended since the original issue was that Playwright passed but real mobile failed.

tests_performed.details: onClick handler triggers handleSave(), toast appears
Emoji picker appears after 700ms hold using pointer events

fix_details.problem: touch-none CSS class blocks all touch events on real mobile devices. Playwright uses mouse events which bypass this.
fix_details.solution: 1) Changed touch-none to touch-manipulation (allows tap/click, prevents scroll/zoom), 2) Simplified save button to use onClick instead of complex touch handlers, 3) Changed reaction button to use unified pointer events (onPointerDown/onPointerUp) which work on both touch and mouse
